section_text: Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 1 of 7 INTRODUCTION The DepartmentofVeteransAffairs(VA)isseeking a partnerwith deep expertise and proven experience indeploying,operating,and managing enterprise - change monitoring solutions.Theprime contractor,nota subcontractor,must demonstr ate a strong trackrecordin implementing secure,scalable,and compliant systemsthatprovide real - time visibilityinto changesacrosscomplex Information Technology( IT ) environments. RequestforInformation (RFI)invitesvendorstosubmit theircapabilitiesand proposed approachesfordelivering a comprehensive Auditand thatsupportsthe VAsoperational,security,and complianceobjectives. VAsOfficeofInformation and Technology,within the InfrastructureOperationspillar, requiresa robustsolution to monitorand auditchangesacrossa wide range ofsystems, including identityand accessmanagementplatforms,file systems,databases,cloud services,and collaboration tools.The selected partnershallprovide fulllifecycle support ongoing operations  with coverage during businesshours,after - hours,weekends,and Federalholidays. DESIREDOUTCOMES INCLUDE: Real - time detection and alerting ofchangesto configurations,permissions,and sensitive data. Centralized,tamper - resistantauditlogging with long - termretentionand secure access controls. Enhanced visibilityinto userand administrative activityacrosshybrid and multi - cloud environments. Seamlessintegration with existing VAsecurityand compliance frameworks,including SecurityInformation and EventManagement ( SIEM ) platforms. Proactive riskmitigation through automated response capabilitiesand forensic - level audittrails. REQUIRED CAPABILITIES INCLUDE: Abilityto trackand controlAdmi ni stratorbehaviorinActive Directoryand Entra ID Deep expertise in enterprise auditing and change monitoring technologies. Experiencewith auditing identitysystems(e.g.,Active Directory),file servers, Abilityto trackand reporton privileged access,group policychanges,and configuration drift. Supportforcompliance with FederalInformation SecurityModernizationAct ( FISMA ) , Health Insurance PortabilityandAccountabilityAct , NationalInstitute of Standardsand Technology ( NIST ) ,and VA - Role - basedaccesscontrol,encryption,and secure log forwarding capabilities. Integration with incidentresponse workflowsand change managementprocesses. The application mustinclude anActive DirectoryGlobalDisasterRecovery capability. Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 2 of 7 PERFORMANCE MEASURES INCLUDE: Meetingorexceeding ServiceLevelAgreements ( SLA ) foralerting,reporting,and issue resolution. Ensuring 99.9%availabilityofthe monitoring platform. Maintainingcurrentsoftware versionsand threatintelligence feeds. Providing continuousmonitoring,documentation,and proactive communication. Fullcompliance with VAchange controlprocessesand regularperformance evaluations. BACKGROUND HARDWARE VAoperatesa complexand distributed ITinfrastructuresupportinga wide range of mission - criticalapplicationsand services.Thisenvironmentincludesenterprise servers, domain controllers,file servers,applicationservers,and networked storage system s located acrossmultiple VAdata centersand field offices.The infrastructure supports both physicaland virtualized systems,with amixofWindows,Linux,and other operating systems. SOFTWARE VAutilizesa diverse setofsoftware platformsand enterprise applicationsthatrequire continuousmonitoring forconfiguration changes,accesscontrolmodifications,and system - levelevents.These include identityand accessmanagementsystems(e.g., Act ive Directory),emailand collaboration platforms,file systems,databases,and custom - builtapplications.Acomprehensive listofmonitored systemsand software componentswillbe provided asan attachmentin the finalsolicitation. AUDITSCOPE The VArequiresa solution thatcan monitorand auditchangesacrossallcritical systems,including butnotlimited to: Userand group modifications Privilegedaccesschanges File and folderpermission changes Group PolicyObject(GPO)changes Systemconfigurationchanges Application - levelchanges Authentication and accessevents SERVICES/LABOR The VAcurrentlysupportsitsauditing and monitoring needsthrough a combination of VA f ull - t ime e mployeesand contractorstaff.Mostpersonneloperate in a remote Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 3 of 7 capacity,with the abilityto provide on - site supportwhen necessaryforsystem upgrades,incidentresponse,orhardware - related interventions. TOUROFDUTY Category Description Day Shift 8:00 AM  4:30 PM,based on the contractorslocaltime zone NormalBusiness Hours Monday  Friday,excluding FederalHolidays CallBack Asrequested,in the eventsupportisneeded outside of normalhours AfterHours Allhoursoutside ofnormalbusinesshours,including weekendsand holidays Holidays FederalHolidaysaspublished bythe U.S.Government Location Allsupportpersonnelmustbe U.S. - based DESIRED OUTCOMES Provide a comprehensive,enterprise - grade Auditand Change Monitoring Solution to manage allsoftware,services,and operationalsupportforVAscurrentand future auditing needs.Performance isrequired during normalbusinesshourswith callback support for after - hoursand criticalevents. 1)SystemMonitoring andAuditLogging(NormalBusiness Hours withCallback) The Contractorshall: Ensure 24x7x365 availabilityofthe auditandmonitoring platformwith 99.9%uptime, with no unplanned outagesaffecting log collection oralerting. Resolve customerissuesand requestswithin 1 businessday usingVAsticketing system(currentlyServiceNow),with callbacksupportasneeded. Maintain auditlogginginfrastructure atpeakefficiencythrough tuning,configuration management,and proactive issue resolution. Ensure allsoftware and hardware componentsare compliantwith VAsecurityand operationalstandards,with upgradescoordinated to avoid downtime. Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 4 of 7 Ensure seamlessintegration with VAsystems,includingidentityplatforms,file servers,databases,and enterprise applications. Provide and maintain up - to - datedocumentation ofsystemconfigurations,audit policies,and operationalprocedures. Supportand maintain the existing Authorityto Operate (ATO) forthe auditsolution. 2)Performance Tuning and Optimization(NormalBusiness Hours withCallback) The Contractorshall: Ensure auditand monitoring toolsoperate efficiently,with no delaysin event collection,correlation,oralerting. Use performance monitoring toolsand automation tomaintain system responsivenessand minimize resource consumption. Continuouslyassessand optimizesystemconfigurationsto supporthigh - volume environmentswithoutdata lossorperformance degradation. 3)Change Tracking and EventCorrelation (NormalBusinessHours with Callback) The Contractorshall: Ensure alltracked changes(e.g.,useraccess,configuration changes,file modifications)are captured and logged in realtime. Provide immediate supportforcustomerrequestsrelated to change tracking, including customreportsoralertconfigurations. Ensure allmonitored systemsare properlyonboardedand configured to supportfull visibilityinto userand systemactivity. 4)SecurityAdministration andAccess Control(NormalBusiness Hourswith Callback) The Contractorshall: Provide 24/7/365 virtualsupport to strengthen the securitypostureofthe auditand monitoring environment. Ensure accessto theauditplatformfollowsthe principleofleastprivilege,with role - based accesscontrolsand audittrailsforalladministrative actions. Align allaccesscontroland securitypolicieswith VAand Federalregulatory standards(e.g., FISMA, NIST). Establish and maintain a clear incidentmanagementprocess,including timelyand accurate reporting ofsecurityincidents. Update securityproceduresregularlyto reflectevolving threatsand compliance requirements. Respond to accesscontrolissueswithin two hours during normalbusinesshours and callbackperiods. Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 5 of 7 5)Software Procurement,Tracking,Installation,andAdministration The Contractorshall: Procure,install,and maintain allsoftware componentsrequired forthe auditand monitoring solution . Validate and tracksoftware usage in coordination withVAapplication ownersand IT stakeholders. Reconcilesoftware licenseswith originalequipmentmanufacturers and VA customersto ensure compliance and costefficiency. Ensure allsoftware toolsare compatible with VAsenterprise systemsand meet TechnicalReference Model(TRM) approvalrequirements. Provide scalabilityto supportadditionalsoftware toolsorremovethose no longer needed. Conductregularanalysisofsoftware usage and optimization levels,identifying opportunitiesto reduce redundancyand improve performance. 6)ProgramManagement,Integration,and Optimization The Contractorshallprovide: Expert - levelknowledge in the configurationand integration ofenterprise auditand monitoring toolsacrossdiverse environments. Experiencewriting andmanagingcustomauditrules,filters,and alerting policies tailored to VAsoperationalneeds. Comprehensive understanding oflog management,eventcorrelation,and forensic analysistechniques. ExpertiseinintegratingwithSIEM platforms, identitysystems(e.g.,ActiveDirectory), and endpointsecuritytools. Proficiencyin scripting and automation (e.g.,PowerShell,Python)to supportcustom reporting and alertingworkflows. Strong communicationand collaboration skillsto workeffectivelywith VAteamsand stakeholders. Experiencewith securitysoftware,including accesscontrolsystemsand SIEM platforms. KnowledgeofFederalauditrequirementsand the abilityto provideevidence of securitycontrolsduring auditsorcompliancereviews. Experiencemanagingdigitalcertificates,keyrings,and secure communications protocols. 7)Performance Measures The Contractorshall: Ensure 99.9%uptimeofthe auditand monitoring infrastructurethatsupportsVAs enterprise systems. Resolve and close service ticketswithin the agreed SLAs,using VAsticketing system (currentlyServiceNow). Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 6 of 7 Maintain alldeployed software atcurrentand supported levels,applying patchesand updatesin accordance with VAchange controlprocedures. Provide continuousmonitoring ofkeysystems,including identityand access managementplatforms,file systems,and databases,with real - timealerting for unauthorized oranomalouschanges. Create andmaintain a centralizedknowledgerepositoryofdocumentation and Standard Operating Procedures(SOPs).Alldocumentation remainsthe propertyof the DepartmentofVeteransAffairs. Monitorprogressagainstagreed - upon milestonesand timelines,and provide regular updatesto VAstakeholders. Periodicallyreviewthe projectschedule and discussaccomplishments,planned activities,and anyissuesorrisksthatmayimpacttaskobjectives. Identifyand proactivelymitigate potentialissuesthatcould affectcontract performance ormission outcomes. Conductregularmeetingsorconference callswith VAto reviewschedule adherence,workquality,and performance metrics. Maintain open and transparentcommunication to addressconcerns,escalate issues,andensure alignmentwith VAgoals. Keep detailed recordsofallmonitoring activities,including progressreports, inspections,and communications. 8)Monitoring and Evaluation The Contractorshall: Adhere to the VAsestablished change controlprocessforallsystem,software,and configuration changes. Conductperiodicperformance and qualityreviewmeetingswith VAstakeholdersto ensure alignmentwith mission objectives. Provide timelyand clearcommunication regarding anyissues,risks,ordeviations fromexpected performance. Establish and maintain an escalation processforunresolved orhigh - impactissues. 9)Forecasted Deliverables The Contractorshallprovide the following deliverableson a recurring oras - needed basis: Monthly TicketResolution Report  SummaryofServiceNowticketactivity, including resolution timelines,troubleshootingsteps,andoutcomes. MonthlyAuditEfficiency Report  Detailsoftuning and optimization effortsto ensure peakperformance oftheauditand monitoring solution. Compliance and Upgrade Documentation  Recordsofallsoftware/hardware complianceactivitiesand upgradecoordination. Integration Documentation  Evidence ofsuccessfulintegrationwith VAsystems and confirmation ofcompatibilitywith existing infrastructure. Requestfor Information A uditand Change Monitoring SolutionRequirements Amendment1 Page 7 of 7 Procedures and SOPs  Up - to - date documentation ofallprocedures,processes, configurations,and SOPs. ATOMaintenance Documentation  Documentation supportingthe maintenance of ATO,including evidence ofcompliance with securitycontrols. Monthly Performance Monitoring Report  Overviewofmonitoring toolsand automationused to ensure systemuptime and performance. ChangeActivityLog  Monthlylog ofalltracked changes,including useractions, systemmodifications,and accesscontrolevents. Monthly Resource Utilization Report  Analysisofsystemresource usage and allocation related to auditand monitoring activities. SupportAvailabilityReport  Documentation of24/7/365 supportavailability, including response timesand supportlogs. Security IncidentReports  Reportsdetailing anysecurityincidents,includingroot cause analysis,response actions,and resolution timelines. Compatibility Documentation  Verificationthatallsoftware toolsare compatible with VAsenterprise systemsand environments. ScalabilityPlans  Documentation outliningthe abilityto scale the solution,add or remove components,and adaptto evolving VAneeds. AuditRule and Policy Documentation  Documentation ofauditrules,filters,and policiesimplementedto meetVAsmonitoring objectives. Monthly Uptime Report  Demonstration ofsystemuptime,with supportingmetrics to validate 99.9%availability. Monitoring Reports  Continuousmonitoring reportsforkeysystems,including identityplatforms,file systems,and databases. Monthly Progress Report  Summaryofaccomplishments,milestonesachieved, upcomingactivities,and anyidentified risksorissues. Software Inventory Report  Detailed inventoryofallsoftware in use,including license types,keys,supportlevels,and compliance with VAsTRM. Software OptimizationReport  Analysisofsoftwareusage,identification of redundanttools,and recommendationsforconsolidation orefficiencyimprovements.

section_text: THIS IS A SOURCES SOUGHT ANNOUNCEMENT ONLY. No Solicitation is currently available. All information contained in this Sources Sought Announcement is preliminary, as well as subject to modification, and is in no way binding on the Government. The Government will not pay for any information submitted in response to this request. The information requested will be used solely within the Department of Veterans Affairs to facilitate decision making and will not be disclosed outside of the Government. The determination of a procurement strategy, based upon the comments submitted in response to this Sources Sought Announcement, is solely within the discretion of the Government. The applicable North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) Code is 811210, Other Electronic and Precision Equipment Repair and Maintenance and the small business size standard is $34.0 Million. The Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) Network Contracting Office (NCO 21) is soliciting information for contractors that can provide Viewpoint Wireless Temperature Monitoring System and Services. A description of services is attached Statement of work (draft). Brief Description of Services See attached Statement of work (draft). Capability Statement All interested contractors must respond by email to Gary.Christensen@va.gov before 1:00 P.M. PDT on September 26, 2024. Please answer the following questions when responding: • What is your company’s Socioeconomic Status? (ex: small business, large business, SDVOSB, etc.) a. If SDVOSB or VOSB, are you registered with the Small Business Administration? Veteran Small Business Certification (sba.gov) • Provide Company’s legal name along with Unique Entity ID (SAM) number.

section_text: Paid Sick Leave for Federal Contractors, applies to all contracts subject to the Service Contract Act for which the contract is awarded (and any solicitation was issued) on or after January 1, 2017. If this contract is covered by the EO, the contractor must provide employees with 1 hour of paid sick leave for every 30 hours they work, up to 56 hours of paid sick leave each year. Employees must be permitted to use paid sick leave for their own illness, injury or other health-related needs, including preventive care; to assist a family member (or person who is like family to the employee) who is ill, injured, or has other health-related needs, including preventive care; or for reasons resulting from, or to assist a family member (or person who is like family to the employee) who is the victim of, domestic violence, sexual assault, or stalking. Additional information on contractor requirements and worker protections under the EO is available at www.dol.gov/whd/govcontracts. section_text: local law, etc.), the cost of furnishing such uniforms and maintaining (by laundering or dry cleaning) such uniforms is an expense that may not be borne by an employee where such cost reduces the hourly rate below that required by the wage determination. The Department of Labor will accept payment in accordance with the following standards as compliance: The contractor or subcontractor is required to furnish all employees with an adequate number of uniforms without cost or to reimburse employees for the actual cost of the uniforms. In addition, where uniform cleaning and maintenance is made the responsibility of the employee, all contractors and subcontractors subject to this wage determination shall (in the absence of a bona fide collective bargaining agreement providing for a different amount, or the furnishing of contrary affirmative proof as to the actual cost), reimburse all employees for such cleaning and maintenance at a rate of $3.35 per week (or $.67 cents per day). However, in those instances where the uniforms furnished are made of "wash and wear" materials, may be routinely washed and dried with other personal garments, and do not require any special treatment such as dry

REQUEST FOR AUTHORIZATION OF ADDITIONAL CLASSIFICATION AND WAGE RATE Standard Form 1444 (SF-1444) Conformance Process: The contracting officer shall require that any class of service employee which is not listed herein and which is to be employed under the contract (i.e., the work to be performed is not performed by any classification listed in the wage determination), be classified by the contractor so as to provide a reasonable relationship (i.e., appropriate level of skill comparison) between such unlisted classifications and the classifications listed in the wage determination. Such conformed classes of employees shall be paid the monetary wages and furnished the fringe benefits as are determined (See 29 CFR 4.6(b)(2)(i)). Such conforming procedures shall be initiated by the contractor prior to the performance of contract work by such unlisted class(es) of employees (See 29 CFR 4.6(b)(2)(ii)).
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: The Wage and Hour Division shall make a final determination of conformed classification, wage rate, and/or fringe benefits which shall be retroactive to the commencement date of the contract (See 29 CFR 4.6(b)(2)(iv)(C)(vi)). When multiple wage determinations are included in a contract, a separate SF-1444 should be prepared for each wage determination to which a class(es) is to be conformed. The process for preparing a conformance request is as follows: 1) When preparing the bid, the contractor identifies the need for a conformed occupation(s) and computes a proposed rate(s). 2) After contract award, the contractor prepares a written report listing in order the proposed classification title(s), a Federal grade equivalency (FGE) for each proposed classification(s), job description(s), and rationale for proposed wage rate(s), including information regarding the agreement or disagreement of the authorized representative of the employees involved, or where there is no authorized representative, the employees themselves. This report should be submitted to the contracting officer no later than 30 days after such unlisted class(es) of employees performs any contract work. 3) The contracting
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: officer reviews the proposed action and promptly submits a report of the action, together with the agency's recommendations and pertinent information including the position of the contractor and the employees, to the Wage and Hour Division, U.S. Department of Labor, for review (See 29 CFR 4.6(b)(2)(ii)). 4) Within 30 days of receipt, the Wage and Hour Division approves, modifies, or disapproves the action via transmittal to the agency contracting officer, or notifies the contracting officer that additional time will be required to process the request. 5) The contracting officer transmits the Wage and Hour decision to the contractor. 6) The contractor informs the affected employees. Information required by the Regulations must be submitted on SF-1444 or bond paper. When preparing a conformance request, the "Service Contract Act Directory of Occupations" (the Directory) should be used to compare job definitions to ensure that duties requested are not performed by a classification already listed in the wage determination. Remember, it is not the job title, but the required tasks that determine whether a class is included in an established wage determination. Conformances may not be used
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: to artificially split, combine, or subdivide classifications listed in the wage determination. ** OCCUPATIONS NOT INCLUDED IN THE SCA DIRECTORY OF OCCUPATIONS ** Kitchen Helper Performs one or more of the following unskilled kitchen duties: Cleans worktables, meat blocks, refrigerator, and grease trays; sweeps, mops kitchen floors; obtains and distributes supplies and utensils; and watches and stirs cooking foods to prevent burning. Carries dirty utensils to be washed and returns cleaned utensils and polished silver to proper place in kitchen. Cleans pots and kitchen utensils. Washes dishes by hand or machine. Carries out garbage. Delivers food trays to patients on resident floor and collects dirty dishes from trays. Assists in setting up trays. Dishes up food. Cuts, peels, and washes fruits and vegetables. Makes toast and beverages. Laundry Worker Performs one or a combination of the nonsupervisory duties required to operate the establishment's laundry facilities. Examples of such duties are: Operating washing or dry-cleaning machines; pressing garment or flat-work by hand or machine; operating an extractor to remove moisture from material; and marking and sorting garments of flat-work.
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: Maid/Porter Cleans and services establishment premises. Performs one or more of the following duties: Cleans, mops, and waxes floors. Dusts furniture and equipment. Cleans window sills, empties trash baskets, and arranges furniture and equipment in an orderly fashion. Scours and polishes, bathtubs, sinks, mirrors, and similar equipment, replenishing supplies of soap and towels. Polishes brass and cleans and polishes glass panels in doors and partitions. Keeps utility storage rooms in good order by cleaning lockers and equipment, arranging supplies, and sweeping and mopping floors. Performs a variety of related duties. Nursing Aide/Orderly Performs routine duties in the care of patients (or residents) usually under the supervision of a registered or practical nurse. Duties involve most of the following: Bathing bed patients or assisting them in bathing; caring for patient's hair and nails; feeding or assisting patients in eating and bringing patients between-meal nourishment; assisting patients with bedpans and urinals; keeping records of patients' food intake and output as directed; assisting patients in dressing and undressing; assisting patients in walking and transporting patients
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: by wheelchair and stretcher; and cleaning and sterilizing instruments and equipment. May clean rooms, make occupied or unoccupied beds, and take and record temperature, pulse, and respiration rate.
title: Open and Continuous Community Nursing Home RFQ 01.01.2026-12.31.2026

section_text: Department of Veterans Affairs Request for Information VA Prescription Drug Monitoring Program (PDMP) Sustainment Support Services VA-26-00013141
title: DA01--State Prescription Drug Monitoring Program (PDMP) Integration Solution and Support Services - New Task Order (VA-26-00013141)

section_text: This Request for Information (RFI) is for planning purposes only and shall not be considered an Invitation for Bid, Request for Task Execution Plan, Request for Quotation or a Request for Proposal. Additionally, there is no obligation on the part of the Government to acquire any products or services described in this RFI. Your response to this RFI will be treated only as information for the Government to consider. You will not be entitled to payment for direct or indirect costs that you incur in responding to this RFI. This request does not constitute a solicitation for proposals or the authority to enter into negotiations to award a contract. No funds have been authorized, appropriated or received for this effort. Interested parties are responsible for adequately marking proprietary, restricted or competition sensitive information contained in their response. The Government does not intend to pay for the information submitted in response to this RFI.
title: DA01--State Prescription Drug Monitoring Program (PDMP) Integration Solution and Support Services - New Task Order (VA-26-00013141)

section_text: This action may be competed under the Transformation Twenty One Total Technology Next Generation multiple award, indefinite delivery, indefinite quantity contract using fair opportunity procedures in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation 16.505. One of the purposes of issuing this RFI is to determine the availability of Small Business Administration (SBA) Veteran Small Business Certification Program (VetCert) Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs) and Veteran Owned Small Businesses (VOSBs) that are capable of providing the services identified in the attached Performance Work Statement (PWS) that are not T4NG Contract holders. An SDVOSB/VOSB company that is not in the SBA certification database (https://veterans/certify.sba.gov/) should not respond to this notice.
title: DA01--State Prescription Drug Monitoring Program (PDMP) Integration Solution and Support Services - New Task Order (VA-26-00013141)

section_text: The North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) for this requirement is 541512 with a size standard of $34 million.
